title: helianthemum montanum var. incanum
intro: Helianthemum montanum var. incanum, also known as Mountain Rockrose, is a plant species in the Cistaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Mountain Rockrose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
